title: How a Glacier Forms
instruction: Put the glacier formation steps in the correct order.
items: Snow builds up. This is called accumulation.
Over hundreds of years, firn becomes glacier ice.
The ice slides downhill due to gravity.

title: Formation of a Corrie (Cirque)
instruction: Arrange the steps in the correct order to explain how a corrie forms.
items: Snow collects in a hollow on the mountainside.
Freeze-thaw weathering breaks up the rock on the valley sides.
The snow compresses into firn and then glacial ice.
A small glacier forms and begins to rotate (rotational slip).
The glacier erodes the hollow through abrasion and plucking.
A armchair-shaped depression called a corrie is formed.
